name: Accessories
description: Crystal accessories and small additions to round out any jewelry box — from spacer beads and cord to display stands and care cloths. These supporting pieces complement the bracelets, rings, and stones across our collections and help you store, wear, and maintain each one. Browse our crystal accessories to find the finishing touches for your everyday pieces.

name: Amazonite Crystal
description: Amazonite crystals are a blue-green to turquoise variety of microcline feldspar named after the Amazon River basin, though most modern material comes from elsewhere. The stone has long been tied to honest communication, courage, and a calm, even temper. Browse our amazonite crystals — beads, palm stones, and freeforms — for that soft teal-green color.

name: Amethyst Crystals
description: Amethyst crystals are the purple variety of quartz, colored by natural irradiation of iron within the stone and mined notably in Brazil and Uruguay. The mineral has long been associated with calm, clear-headedness, and steady focus, which is why it appears so often in meditation pieces and bedside decor. Shop our amethyst crystals — bracelets, spheres, towers, and clusters — to bring that violet tone into your collection.

name: Angelite Crystals
description: Angelite crystals are a soft blue form of anhydrite (3 to 3.5 Mohs) that earned the name from its pale, sky-like color. The mineral should be kept dry, as exposure to moisture can change its surface over time. The stone has a long association with calm, gentle expression, and a quiet, even presence. Browse our angelite crystals — carved figures, beads, and palm stones — for that soft blue.

name: Apatite Crystals
description: Apatite crystals are a phosphate mineral that spans blue, blue-green, and yellow tones, with the vivid blue variety most sought after. At 5 on the Mohs scale it’s softer than quartz, so it’s worn and handled with care. The stone has a long association with motivation, creativity, and clear self-expression. Browse our apatite crystals — beads, cabochons, and raw pieces — for that electric blue-green.

name: Aventurine Crystals
description: Aventurine crystals are a quartz whose included mica particles throw a soft sparkle called aventurescence across a green (most common), blue, or peach surface. The stone has a long-standing association with opportunity, growth, and a confident, forward-looking outlook. Shop our aventurine crystals — tumbled stones, beads, and palm stones — for that gentle glinting green.

name: Birthstone Necklaces
description: Birthstone necklaces pair each month with its signature gem, turning a date into something wearable. January’s garnet, February’s amethyst, May’s emerald-green alternative — every pendant carries the lore and color tied to its month, making these pieces natural gifts for birthdays and milestones. Shop our birthstone necklaces to find the right stone for yourself or someone you celebrate.

name: Black Tourmaline Crystals
description: Black tourmaline crystals are a dark, columnar mineral (schorl) that forms in long vertical striations and ranks around 7 to 7.5 on the Mohs scale. The stone is widely regarded as a grounding and protective mineral, the one many reach for at thresholds and entryways. Shop our black tourmaline crystals — from raw chunks to polished bracelets — for a steady, dark presence.

name: Bloodstone Crystals
description: Bloodstone crystals are a dark green chalcedony scattered with red spots of iron oxide — the speckling that gave the stone its name in antiquity. A hard, durable mineral (6.5 to 7 Mohs), bloodstone carries a long association with courage, vitality, and strength. Browse our bloodstone crystals — tumbled stones, beads, and carved pieces — for that dark green and red contrast.

name: By Shape / Function
description: Crystals by shape and function let you pick the form a crystal takes before the stone itself — spheres, towers, hearts, pyramids, tumbled stones, malas and more. Each shape carries its own use, from a meditation focal point to a pocket worry stone, so the format often drives how the piece fits your day. Browse crystals by shape and function to match the right form to your purpose.

name: By Stone
description: Shopping by stone lets you follow the mineral rather than the format. Here every crystal in the catalog is grouped by its identity — amethyst, rose quartz, labradorite, jade and dozens more — so you can compare how one stone appears across bracelets, spheres, towers, and tumbled pieces. Explore crystals by stone to read what each mineral has long been associated with and find your match.

name: Carnelian Crystals
description: Carnelian crystals are a chalcedony quartz in warm orange to reddish-brown tones, colored by iron oxide and sourced notably from India and Madagascar. The stone has a long history as a talisman for courage, motivation, and getting started on hard things. Shop our carnelian crystals — beads, palm stones, and towers — for that warm, encouraging color.

name: Chakra Bracelets & Stones
description: Chakra bracelets and stones reference the body’s seven chakras as described in yogic tradition, using seven differently colored minerals arranged in sequence along the strand or grid. Each color stands in for one of the seven centers in that cultural framework, rather than making any claim about the body itself. Browse our chakra bracelets and stones for pieces rooted in that tradition.

name: Chrysocolla Crystals
description: Chrysocolla crystals are a soft blue-green copper mineral (2 to 4 Mohs) often found intergrown with quartz, which gives it flashes of turquoise and teal across a mottled surface. The stone has a long association with calm communication and honest expression. Shop our chrysocolla crystals — cabochons, beads, and freeforms — for that water-toned color.

name: Citrine Crystals
description: Citrine crystals are the yellow to golden-orange variety of quartz, colored by trace iron, and one of the stones most often tied to abundance and a confident, forward-looking outlook. Natural citrine is rarer than the heat-treated amethyst sold under the same name, so the source matters. Shop our citrine crystals — points, beads, and clusters — for that warm golden tone.

name: Clear Quartz Crystals
description: Clear quartz crystals are silicon dioxide in its purest, most transparent form — the same mineral family that includes amethyst, citrine, and rose quartz. Large natural points form with six sides terminating in a single tip, and the stone has a long association with clarity, focus, and amplifying intention. Shop our clear quartz crystals — points, clusters, and spheres — for that clean, glass-clear look.

name: Copper Jewelry
description: Copper jewelry is built around the warm metal itself — rings, cuffs, and beads handworked using techniques drawn from Tibetan and South Asian metalwork traditions. Each piece carries the maker’s hand and the patina copper develops over time, so the metal ages with the wearer. Browse our copper jewelry for handworked pieces rooted in that craft heritage.

image.name: Om Mani Padme Hum Copper Ring
image.alt: Om Mani Padme Hum Copper Ring

name: Crystal Angels
description: Crystal angels are carvings that shape the stone into a winged figure long used as a symbol of guardianship and protection across spiritual traditions. The carved wings and form carry that symbolic meaning, while the mineral underneath adds its own color. Browse our crystal angels for carved guardian figures in genuine stone.

name: Crystal Anklet
description: Crystal anklets bring stone to the ankle, a less common placement that reads as deliberate and understated. Small beads and thin cord keep the strand light enough for the lower leg, and the mineral shows with every step. Shop our crystal anklets for beaded strands sized to wrap the ankle.

name: Crystal Bracelets
description: Crystal bracelets cover the full range of wrist-worn stone — beaded strands, chip bracelets, metal-and-stone cuffs, and themed designs grouped by zodiac or intention. Whatever the build, the mineral stays genuine and visible. Shop our crystal bracelets to find the construction and stone that matches how you like to wear it.

name: Crystal Cubes
description: Crystal cubes cut the stone into a solid block with six equal faces — a form that reads as stable, grounded, and architectural. The right angles show the mineral’s internal pattern in cross-section, and the weight sits firmly wherever it’s placed. Browse our crystal cubes for carved pieces built on a steady, square form.

name: Crystal Earrings
description: Crystal earrings frame the face with stone, catching light with every turn of the head. Studs, drops, and point settings each sit differently on the ear, and the mineral’s color reads strongest against skin at this distance. Shop our crystal earrings to find the stone and setting that suits your ear and everyday look.

image.name: Fair Trade Lunar Crescent Earrings – Gold Tone
image.alt: Fair Trade Lunar Crescent Earrings - Gold Tone

name: Crystal Flames
description: Crystal flames are carved into a tapered, flame-like form that twists and rises — narrower than a freeform, softer than a tower. The carved silhouette catches light along its curves and reads as movement frozen in stone. Browse our crystal flames for carved pieces with that rising, flame-shaped contour.

name: Crystal Ganesh
description: Crystal Ganesh carvings depict the elephant-headed figure revered across Hindu and yogic tradition, carved from a single piece of stone. The form carries the cultural and devotional meaning of the deity it represents, and the mineral underneath adds its own color and character. Browse our crystal Ganesh carvings for devotional pieces in genuine stone.

name: Crystal Hearts
description: Crystal hearts shape the stone into the universal symbol of affection and emotion, which is why they’re so often chosen as gifts and kept on a nightstand or shelf. The carved form softens the mineral while keeping its natural color and banding visible. Shop our crystal hearts for carved pieces that carry a clear sentiment.

name: Crystal Malas
description: Crystal malas are strands of 108 beads rooted in Buddhist and Hindu practice, where they’re used to count recitations and breath. Each bead is large enough to move one at a time between the fingers, and the mineral runs the full length of the strand. Browse our crystal malas for 108-bead strands built for that counting practice.

name: Crystal Mekabas
description: Crystal mekabas (star tetrahedrons) are carved into a star tetrahedron — two interlocking triangular pyramids forming a three-dimensional star, a shape drawn from sacred geometry. The form is built around its geometry rather than any single use, and it sits as a striking object on a desk or grid. Browse our crystal merkabas for star-tetrahedron carvings in genuine stone.

name: Crystal Necklaces
description: Crystal necklaces bring stone to the neckline, where a pendant sits visible through the day. Polished points, raw chunks, and beaded strands each change how the mineral reads against skin and clothing, and the length sets whether it rests at the collar or lower. Shop our crystal necklaces to find the pendant, stone, and length that fits your neckline.

image.name: Large Amethyst Crystal on Beaded Iolite Necklace
image.alt: Large Amethyst Crystal on Beaded Iolite Necklace

name: Crystal Pyramids
description: Crystal pyramids cut the stone into four triangular faces rising to a single apex — one of the oldest geometric forms, long used in sacred architecture across cultures. The shape catches light along its edges and sits stable on any flat surface. Browse our crystal pyramids for carved geometric pieces with a steady base.

name: Crystal Rings
description: Crystal rings set genuine stone into a format you see every time you move your hand. Polished cabochons, raw nuggets, and faceted points each catch the light differently, and the band keeps the mineral fixed where you can glance at it. Shop our crystal rings to find the stone, setting, and size that fits your hand.

name: Crystal Spheres
description: Crystal spheres are carved into a perfect ball, a shape with no beginning or edge, which is part of why they’re used as a steady focal point for meditation or a centerpiece on a desk. Polished smooth, a sphere shows the stone’s color and pattern evenly from every angle. Shop our crystal spheres to find the mineral and size that grounds your space.

image.name: Golden Obsidian Gemstone Sphere – 1 of a Kind
image.alt: Golden Obsidian Gemstone Sphere - 1 of a Kind

name: Crystal Towers
description: Crystal towers, also called obelisks or generators, are carved into a tall form that rises to a single pointed termination. The vertical shape directs the eye and the light upward, and the solid base keeps the piece standing tall on a shelf or altar. Shop our crystal towers for tall, pointed pieces that anchor a surface.

image.name: Red Jasper Crystal Point – AAA Premium Quality
image.alt: Red Jasper Crystal Point - AAA Premium Quality

name: Crystals Stones
description: Crystals and stones – sorted by mineral or by shape – make up the core of the catalog — every genuine piece of natural stone we carry, from a single tumbled pebble to a large carved tower. Browse by the stone you want or the form it takes, and read what each mineral has long been associated with along the way. Shop crystals and stones across the full range of formats and minerals.

name: Freeform Crystals
description: Freeform crystals are carved along the natural contours of the rough stone rather than into a fixed shape like a sphere or tower. The result is a one-of-a-kind polished piece whose curves follow the mineral itself — no two share the same silhouette. Shop our freeform crystals for individual carved pieces that don’t repeat.

name: Gemstone Bracelet Sets
description: Gemstone bracelet sets take the guesswork out of stacking by pairing stones whose colors and meanings read well together. Curated combinations of two, three, or more strands let you layer complementary minerals in one go, whether for contrast or a tonal gradient. Browse our gemstone bracelet sets for ready-made stacks that work straight from the box.

name: Gemstone Bracelets
description: Gemstone bracelets turn natural stone into the most worn format of all — a strand of polished beads that sits on the wrist all day. From single-stone classics to mixed beaded designs, each bracelet keeps the mineral’s true color and weight. Shop our gemstone bracelets to find the stone and bead size that suits your wrist and your stack.

name: Gemstone Bracelets 8mm
description: 8mm gemstone bracelets hit the middle ground that most wearers settle on — substantial enough to feel the stone, slim enough to layer or wear alone. These gemstone bracelets use a consistent 8mm bead size across every stone, so mixing amethyst with tiger eye or rose quartz reads as one even stack. Shop our 8mm gemstone bracelets for a balanced, stackable fit.

name: Gold & Silver Bracelets
description: Gold and silver bracelets pair genuine stone beads with metal accents — gold-tone or silver-tone spacers, charms, and hardware that frame the mineral. The metal changes how the stone’s color reads: warm tones with gold, cooler stones with silver. Shop our gold and silver bracelets to find the metal-and-stone combination that suits your stack.

name: Healing Crystals Jewelry
description: Healing crystals jewelry is where meaning meets the everyday — bracelets, rings, necklaces, and earrings cut from genuine stone rather than glass or resin. Each piece keeps the natural color and character of the mineral it’s made from, so the qualities long linked to that stone travel with you. Browse our healing crystals jewelry collection to find the format and stone that fits your routine.

name: Hematite Crystals
description: Hematite crystals are an iron oxide with a signature steel-gray to black metallic surface that leaves a red streak when drawn across unglazed porcelain. Dense and noticeably heavy for its size, hematite has long been regarded as a grounding and steadying mineral. Browse our hematite crystals — tumbled stones, beads, and magnets — for that cool metallic weight.

name: Herkimer Diamond
description: Herkimer diamonds are double-terminated quartz crystals found almost exclusively in Herkimer County, New York — clear, colorless points with natural facets on both ends, which is why they’re sold as diamonds despite being quartz. The mineral has a long association with clarity, attunement, and sharp focus. Browse our Herkimer diamond pieces for naturally faceted double-terminated points.

name: Jade Crystals
description: Jade crystals refer to two distinct minerals — nephrite and jadeite — that share a tough, interlocking structure and a range of green, lavender, white, and honey tones. Prized across East Asian culture for millennia, jade has a long association with harmony, wisdom, and good fortune. Shop our jade crystals — carvings, beads, and palm stones — for that dense, waxy green.

name: Kyanite Crystals
description: Kyanite crystals form as long, bladed minerals most often in blue, prized for their striking striated columns. Notably, kyanite has two different hardnesses depending on direction (4.5 to 7 Mohs), a property that sets it apart from most stones. The mineral carries a long association with clear communication and calm focus. Browse our kyanite crystals — blades, beads, and rough pieces — for that deep blue.

name: Labradorite Crystals
description: Labradorite crystals are a feldspar whose internal structure throws flashes of blue, gold, and green across a grey-black body as the stone moves — an effect called labradorescence, found in material from Madagascar and Canada. The stone has a long association with transformation, intuition, and navigating change. Shop our labradorite crystals — freeforms, beads, and towers — for that moving flash.

name: Lapis Lazuli Crystals
description: Lapis lazuli crystals are a deep blue rock made mainly of lazurite, flecked with gold pyrite and white calcite — the same stone ground into ultramarine pigment for Renaissance paintings, historically mined in Afghanistan. It carries a long association with wisdom, truth, and inner vision. Shop our lapis lazuli crystals — cabochons, beads, and carved pieces — for that deep celestial blue.

name: Larimar Crystals
description: Larimar crystals are a blue variety of pectolite found only in the Dominican Republic, ranging from sky white to deep ocean blue with white patterning that recalls water. A relatively soft stone (4.5 to 5 Mohs), larimar carries a long association with calm, emotional steadiness, and clear communication. Browse our larimar crystals — cabochons, beads, and pendants — for that Caribbean blue.

image.name: Rare Genuine Larimar Crystal Point Earrings
image.alt: Rare Genuine Larimar Crystal Point Earrings

name: Lepidolite Crystals
description: Lepidolite crystals are a mica that owes its lavender to purple color to natural lithium content, often found in scaly, flaky masses. A soft mineral (2.5 to 4 Mohs), lepidolite has a long association with calm, emotional steadiness, and moving through transitions. Browse our lepidolite crystals — beads, palm stones, and rough pieces — for that soft lilac tone.

name: Malachite Crystals
description: Malachite crystals are a copper carbonate that bands into concentric rings of bright and dark green, often mined in the Democratic Republic of Congo. A relatively soft stone (3.5 to 4 Mohs), malachite carries a long association with transformation and protection — handle with care and keep it dry, as it can be affected by moisture. Shop our malachite crystals — cabochons, beads, and freeforms.

name: Obsidian Crystals
description: Obsidian crystals aren’t a true crystal at all but a natural volcanic glass, cooled so fast that no crystal structure forms — which gives it a conchoidal fracture and a razor edge used for tools since the Stone Age. The stone carries a long association with protection, truth, and grounding. Shop our obsidian crystals — spheres, mirrors, and beads — for that deep, glassy black.

name: Opal Crystals
description: Opal crystals are hydrated silica whose internal spheres diffract light into flashes of color across the stone — the play-of-color that makes precious opal so distinctive. Material ranges from white and black to fire opal, each with its own flash pattern. The stone carries a long association with hope, creativity, and emotional expression. Shop our opal crystals — cabochons, beads, and pendants.

name: Prehnite Crystals
description: Prehnite crystals are a pale green to yellow-green calcium-aluminum silicate, often translucent with a soft, waxy surface and occasional inclusions of epidote or rutile. The mineral carries a long association with calm, decluttering, and a quiet, caring presence. Browse our prehnite crystals — cabochons, beads, and freeforms — for that soft pistachio green.

name: Pyrite Crystals
description: Pyrite crystals are an iron sulfide with a brass-yellow metallic gleam that earned it the nickname fool’s gold, often forming in perfect cubes or disc-like nodules. The mineral has a long association with abundance, confidence, and a steady, motivated mindset. Shop our pyrite crystals — cubes, clusters, and tumbled pieces — for that bright metallic gold.

name: Rainbow Fluorite Crystals
description: Rainbow fluorite crystals are calcium fluoride (4 Mohs) that bands into purple, green, blue, and yellow zones within a single specimen, which is where the rainbow name comes from. The stone has a long association with focus, clear thinking, and working through complex tasks. Shop our rainbow fluorite crystals — octahedrons, towers, and spheres — for that layered color.

name: Rainbow Moonstone Crystals
description: Rainbow moonstone crystals are a variety of feldspar whose internal layering scatters light into a blue-white flash that moves across the surface as the stone turns — an effect called adularescence. The mineral has long been associated with intuition, new beginnings, and calm. Browse our rainbow moonstone crystals — cabochons, beads, and pendants — for that moving blue sheen.

name: Raw Crystal Necklace
description: A raw crystal necklace keeps the pendant unpolished, holding the natural edges and inclusions the stone grew with. Because rough pieces form individually, no two pendants share the same silhouette — the necklace reads as a single found object rather than a cast shape. Browse our raw crystal necklaces for uncut pendants in their natural state.

name: Raw Crystal Rings
description: Raw crystal rings keep the stone unpolished, holding on to the natural facets and texture it grew with. Each piece sits slightly differently because no two rough stones form alike, which is the appeal — a ring that doesn’t look stamped out. Browse our raw crystal rings for one-of-a-kind settings in their natural state.

name: Red Jasper Crystals
description: Red jasper crystals are an opaque chalcedony in brick-red to terracotta tones, colored by iron and often banded or speckled with darker markings. A hard, durable stone (6.5 to 7 Mohs), red jasper has a long association with grounding, stamina, and steady endurance. Shop our red jasper crystals — palm stones, beads, and towers — for that warm, earthy red.

name: Rhodonite Crystals
description: Rhodonite crystals are a manganese silicate in pink to rose-red, patterned with black veins of manganese oxide that web across the surface — the contrast the name (from the Greek for rose) refers to. The stone carries a long association with compassion, forgiveness, and a steady sense of self-worth. Browse our rhodonite crystals — beads, palm stones, and cabochons — for that rose-and-black patterning.

name: Rose Quartz Crystals
description: Rose quartz crystals are the pink variety of quartz, colored by trace titanium and manganese, ranging from a faint wash to a deep rosy tone. The stone has one of the longest-running associations in crystal lore — with love, compassion, and care for oneself and others. Shop our rose quartz crystals — hearts, beads, and towers — for that soft, universal pink.

name: Ruby Crystals
description: Ruby crystals are the red variety of the mineral corundum, colored by chromium, and at 9 on the Mohs scale they’re second only to diamond in hardness — the classic source is Myanmar’s Mogok region. The stone carries a long association with passion, courage, and vitality. Shop our ruby crystals — beads, cabochons, and raw pieces — for that deep, saturated red.

name: Selenite Crystals
description: Selenite crystals are a translucent form of gypsum that earns the name from selene, the Greek word for moon, thanks to its pale luminous surface. At just 2 on the Mohs scale it’s soft enough to scratch with a fingernail, so it’s kept as a display piece rather than worn hard. Browse our selenite crystals — towers, wands, and logs — for that moonlit, fibrous glow.

name: Serpentine Crystals
description: Serpentine crystals take their name from the serpent-like mottling across their green, yellow-green, and black surface. A relatively soft mineral (2.5 to 3.5 Mohs), serpentine has long been linked to grounding and to navigating change. Browse our serpentine crystals for carved and tumbled pieces in those earthy green tones.

name: Shungite Crystals
description: Shungite crystals are a black, carbon-rich stone found almost exclusively in Karelia, Russia, notable for its fullerenes — molecular carbon cages rare in nature. The mineral has a modern reputation for purification and a steady, grounded presence. Browse our shungite crystals — tumbled stones, pyramids, and elite nuggets — for that deep matte black.

name: Spiritual Necklace
description: Spiritual necklaces carry symbols and stones long tied to practice — mandalas, lotus forms, rudraksha, and pendants cut from minerals associated with focus or calm. They sit close to the chest through the day, which is part of why this format is so often chosen for pieces meant to mean something. Shop our spiritual necklaces for designs that hold a intention you want near.

image.name: Large Amethyst Crystal on Beaded Iolite Necklace
image.alt: Large Amethyst Crystal on Beaded Iolite Necklace

name: Stud Earrings
description: Stud earrings keep a single stone close to the ear, where it reads as a small point of color rather than a full design. Polished cabochons and faceted gems both work in this minimal setting, which is why studs are the most worn earring format day to day. Shop our crystal stud earrings for clean, low-profile stone at the ear.

image.name: Pink Opal Silver or Gold Stud Earrings
image.alt: Pink Opal Silver or Gold Stud Earrings

name: Tiger Eye Crystals
description: Tiger eye crystals are a chatoyant quartz whose parallel fibers throw a single shifting band of light across a golden-brown surface as the stone turns — the cat’s-eye effect the name comes from. The stone has a long-standing association with courage, confidence, and a grounded sense of will. Shop our tiger eye crystals — beads, towers, and palm stones — for that warm, moving shimmer.

name: Tumbled Stones
description: Tumbled stones are small pieces of raw mineral smoothed in a rock tumbler until every edge rounds off, leaving a palm-sized pebble with a glossy face. The format is built for carrying — pockets, pouches, bowls, and grids — and lets you keep a mineral close without the weight of a larger piece. Shop our tumbled stones for pocket pebbles in dozens of minerals.

name: Turquoise Crystals
description: Turquoise crystals are a copper-aluminum phosphate that ranges from sky blue to green, with dark matrix veining from the host rock it forms in. One of the oldest stones in human adornment, turquoise carries a long history as a protective and friendship stone across cultures from the American Southwest to Persia. Shop our turquoise crystals — cabochons, beads, and inlay pieces.

name: Worry Stones
description: Worry stones are shaped to fit the curve of a thumb, with a smooth polished face and a gentle indent meant for rubbing. The format is built for the hand and the pocket — small enough to carry, shaped for an absent, repetitive motion. Shop our worry stones for polished thumb stones in the minerals you reach for most.

name: Zodiac Bracelets
description: Zodiac bracelets pair each sign of the western zodiac with the stones traditionally linked to it — a way to carry your sign on the wrist. Each design maps a constellation to its associated minerals, so Aries, Scorpio, Pisces and the rest each have a dedicated bead combination. Shop our zodiac bracelets to find the strand that matches your sign.
